title: How the AI Bubble Bursts
content: How the AI bubble bursts | Volpe’s Blog Volpe's Blog My points of view, travels and code en Picture by Bloomberg The catalysts for a crash are already laid out, and it can happen sooner than most expect. AI is here to stay. If used right, chances are it will make us all more productive. That, on the other hand, does not mean it will be a good investment. Big tech doesn’t need to win, just outspend Magnificent 7 companies are increasing capex to their biggest ever to differentiate their tech from each other and the big AI labs, but the key realization is that they don’t have to spend it to win. It’s a defensive move for them, if they commit $50B, OpenAI and Anthropic need to go raise $100B each to stay competitive, which makes them reliant on investors’ money. As the numbers get bigger, the amount of funds that can write checks of the size required to fill such amounts gets smaller. And many of them are now getting bombed in the Gulf . This is the reason there’s a push for IPOs , it’s because it’s the only option left to keep the funding coming. Taking this into account, Google is extremely well positioned to weather the storm. When they announce capex expenditure, they don’t spend it overnight. They can simply deploy month by month until their competitors struggle to raise and get forced to capitulate. At that point they can just ramp down the spending and declare victory in a cornered market. They don’t need capex, they just need to make it very clear for everyone that nobody can outspend them. It is hard to picture as numbers get so big, but Alphabet (Google’s parent) is ten times more valuable than the biggest military company 1 . This also has a great implication for the Mag 7, especially Google: their capex will be a lot smaller in practice than projected, and as investors hate to see high capex in tech, the market will probably reward that if it materializes. Apple didn’t even have to pretend, their strategy of waiting on the sidelines, while selling Mac Minis, for someone to come up with a good-enough model and just buy that when it’s done seems to be working. They may not even do that, they are now hinting at charging models for being available on Siri . Amazon is hedged with an Anthropic investment, and Meta is spending like there’s no tomorrow. The catalyzer We’re hitting the worst-case scenarios for the big AI labs: energy, their biggest expense, is at multi-year highs , capital from the Gulf is not available for obvious reasons , there are serious concerns about a rate hike , and RAM prices are crashing because new models won’t need as much , but labs already bought them at sky-high prices . And that last innovation came from their biggest competitor, Google. Anthropic is already in a push to reduce costs and increase revenue . If investor money dries up, they will be forced to cut their losses and pass the true costs to their users. The question is now if customers will be willing to pay up. Independent reports state that Claude mete
title_zh: 人工智能泡沫如何破裂
summary_zh: AI泡沫如何破裂：催化剂已就位，崩溃可能比预期更早到来，尽管AI技术将长期存在并提升生产力，但这并不意味着它是一项好的投资。当前科技巨头正陷入一场资本消耗战，其核心逻辑并非赢得竞争，而是通过巨额支出拖垮对手。，以“美股七巨头”为代表的科技公司正将资本支出（capex）增至历史最高水平，旨在与竞争对手及OpenAI、Anthropic等大型AI实验室拉开技术差距。然而关键在于，它们无需依靠这些支出获胜，这本质上是一种防御性策略。例如，若谷歌承诺投入500亿美元，OpenAI和Anthropic就需各自筹集1000亿美元才能保持竞争力，从而使其深度依赖投资者资金。随着金额攀升，有能力提供如此规模资金的机构越来越少，而许多海湾地区投资者目前正面临困境。这迫使AI公司寻求IPO，因为这是维持资金流的唯一途径。，谷歌在此形势下占据绝对优势。其资本支出并非一次性投入，而是可逐月部署，静待竞争对手因融资困难而被迫退出。届时谷歌便可减少开支，在垄断市场中宣告胜利。其母公司Alphabet的市值是最大军工企业的十倍，这使其具备无与伦比的财务耐力。对“七巨头”（尤其是谷歌）而言，实际资本支出可能远低于预期，若成真，市场或将给予积极反馈。，各巨头策略分化：苹果采取观望态度，一边销售Mac Mini，一边等待成熟模型出现以便直接收购，甚至可能转向对集成至Siri的模型收费；亚马逊通过投资Anthropic对冲风险；Meta则在不计成本地投入。，当前AI实验室正面临最坏情况：能源成本（最大支出项）处于多年高位；海湾资本因故不可用；加息风险加剧；RAM价格因新模型需求减少而暴跌，但实验室此前已以高价采购——这项降低内存需求的创新恰恰来自其最大竞争对手谷歌。Anthropic已开始推行降本增收。若投资者资金枯竭，实验室将被迫削减损失并向用户转嫁真实成本，但客户是否愿意买单仍是未知数。，独立报告显示，Claude等模型的商业吸引力有限。当资本盛宴结束，只有具备真正产品市场契合度、能实现现金流自给的企业才能存活，届时AI行业将迎来残酷的出清时刻。

title: Mathematical methods and human thought in the age of AI
content: [2603.26524] Mathematical methods and human thought in the age of AI Mathematics > History and Overview arXiv:2603.26524 (math) [Submitted on 27 Mar 2026] Title: Mathematical methods and human thought in the age of AI Authors: Tanya Klowden , Terence Tao View a PDF of the paper titled Mathematical methods and human thought in the age of AI, by Tanya Klowden and 1 other authors View PDF HTML (experimental) Abstract: Artificial intelligence (AI) is the name popularly given to a broad spectrum of computer tools designed to perform increasingly complex cognitive tasks, including many that used to solely be the province of humans. As these tools become exponentially sophisticated and pervasive, the justifications for their rapid development and integration into society are frequently called into question, particularly as they consume finite resources and pose existential risks to the livelihoods of those skilled individuals they appear to replace. In this paper, we consider the rapidly evolving impact of AI to the traditional questions of philosophy with an emphasis on its application in mathematics and on the broader real-world outcomes of its more general use. We assert that artificial intelligence is a natural evolution of human tools developed throughout history to facilitate the creation, organization, and dissemination of ideas, and argue that it is paramount that the development and application of AI remain fundamentally human-centered. With an eye toward innovating solutions to meet human needs, enhancing the human quality of life and expanding the capacity for human thought and understanding, we propose a pathway to integrating AI into our most challenging and intellectually rigorous fields to the benefit of all humankind. Comments: 27 pages. This is an unabridged version of an article solicited for the Blackwell Companion to the Philosophy of Mathematics Subjects: History and Overview (math.HO) MSC classes: 01A67 Cite as: arXiv:2603.26524 [math.HO] (or arXiv:2603.26524v1 [math.HO] for this version) https://doi.org/10.48550/arXiv.2603.26524 Focus to learn more arXiv-issued DOI via DataCite (pending registration) Submission history From: Terence C. title_zh: 人工智能时代的数学方法与人类思维
summary_zh: 在人工智能时代，数学方法与人类思维，人工智能（AI）作为一系列旨在执行日益复杂认知任务的计算机工具的总称，正以前所未有的速度和广度融入社会。随着AI工具变得极其复杂和普及，其快速发展与社会整合的合理性也受到质疑，尤其是考虑到它们消耗有限资源，并对可能被替代的专业人士的生存构成风险。本文由Tanya Klowden和陶哲轩（Terence Tao）撰写，探讨了AI对传统哲学问题的快速演变的影响，重点聚焦于其在数学领域的应用以及更广泛使用所带来的现实世界结果。，作者认为，人工智能是人类历史上为促进思想创造、组织和传播而开发的工具的自然演进。他们强调，AI的发展和应用必须坚持以人为本的基本原则。论文主张，AI的整合应着眼于创新解决方案以满足人类需求、提升人类生活质量，并扩展人类思维和理解的能力。，文章特别关注数学领域，探讨了AI如何影响数学方法与实践。作者提出了一条将AI整合到最具挑战性和智力严谨的领域（如数学）的路径，旨在造福全人类。这篇长达27页的论文是应《布莱克威尔数学哲学指南》邀约而撰写的完整版本，其核心论点是：在AI时代，技术的发展必须服务于增强人类思维与福祉，确保技术进步与人类价值和谐共存。

title: Police used AI facial recognition to wrongly arrest TN woman for crimes in ND
content: Police used AI facial recognition to arrest a Tennessee woman for crimes committed in a state she says she’s never visited | CNN US 8 min read Police used AI facial recognition to arrest a Tennessee woman for crimes committed in a state she says she’s never visited By Zoe Sottile Updated Mar 29, 2026, 6:00 AM ET Published Mar 29, 2026, 6:00 AM ET PUBLISHED Mar 29, 2026, 6:00 AM ET Angela Lipps, 50, was first arrested in Tennessee on July 14, according to a statement from the Fargo Police Department and a verified GoFundMe page. KVRR; Cass County Sheriff's Office AI See all topics Facebook Tweet Email Link Threads Link Copied! Follow A Tennessee grandmother spent more than five months in jail after police used an AI facial recognition tool to link her to crimes committed in North Dakota – a state she says she’d never been to before. Police in Fargo, North Dakota, have acknowledged “a few errors” in the case and pledged changes in their operations but stopped short of issuing a direct apology. Angela Lipps, 50, was first arrested in Tennessee on July 14, according to a statement from the Fargo Police Department and a verified GoFundMe for Lipps. Unbeknownst to Lipps, a warrant had been issued for her arrest weeks earlier – in Fargo, over 1,000 miles away from her Tennessee home. Months before, several instances of bank fraud had occurred in and around Fargo, according to police. In their search for a suspect in the bank fraud cases, investigators used “our partner agency’s facial recognition technology” as well as “additional investigative steps independent of AI to assist in identification” before submitting the report to the Cass County State Attorney’s Office, Fargo Police Department Chief Dave Zibolski told CNN in an email. But Zibolski said at a Tuesday news conference that his police department’s reliance on some of the information from a neighboring agency’s AI system is “part of the issue,” referring to errors made in Lipps’ case. “At some point, our partner agency over at West Fargo purchased their own AI facial recognition system that we were not aware of at the executive level …, and we would not have allowed that to be used, and it has since been prohibited,” he said. The West Fargo Police Department told CNN that they use Clearview AI , a startup with a database of billions of photos scraped from the internet, including social media. Clearview “identified a potential suspect with similar features to Angela Lipps” and West Fargo police shared that report with Fargo police, reads a statement from the police department. The statement notes that West Fargo police didn’t forward any charges and didn’t have enough evidence to charge anyone for the fraud case in West Fargo. CNN has reached out to Clearview AI for comment. It’s unclear what other evidence was used in the investigation to tie Lipps to the crimes. Lipps’ case comes as police departments across the country have rapidly integrated new technologies, including AI. But police use of
title_zh: 警方使用AI面部识别技术误捕田纳西州女子 指控其在北达科他州犯罪
summary_zh: 美国田纳西州一名50岁的祖母安吉拉·利普斯因AI人脸识别技术错误匹配，被指控在从未到访过的北达科他州犯下银行欺诈罪，并因此被错误监禁超过五个月。法戈市警方承认案件存在“一些错误”并承诺改进操作，但未直接道歉。，根据法戈警察局声明及利普斯 verified GoFundMe 页面信息，利普斯于7月14日在田纳西州首次被捕。她对此毫不知情，实际上早在数周前，距离其住所1000多英里外的法戈市已对她签发了逮捕令。警方称，此前法戈及周边地区发生了多起银行欺诈案。，法戈警察局长戴夫·齐博尔斯基向CNN透露，调查人员在侦办欺诈案时，使用了“合作机构的人脸识别技术”以及“独立于AI的额外调查步骤”进行嫌疑人识别，随后将报告提交至卡斯县州检察官办公室。但在周二的新闻发布会上，齐博尔斯基指出，警方依赖来自相邻机构AI系统的部分信息是“问题的一部分”，这直接导致了利普斯案的错误。，他进一步说明，西法戈警察局在某个时间点自行采购了AI人脸识别系统，而法戈警方高层此前并不知情，并表示“我们本不会允许使用该系统，现已禁止此类使用”。西法戈警察局向CNN证实，他们使用的是Clearview AI。这家初创公司拥有从互联网（包括社交媒体）抓取的数十亿张照片数据库。西法戈警方声明称，Clearview AI“识别出一名与安吉拉·利普斯特征相似的可能嫌疑人”，并将该报告分享给了法戈警方。声明强调，西法戈警方并未提出任何指控，且没有足够证据就西法戈的欺诈案起诉任何人。CNN已联系Clearview AI寻求评论。，目前尚不清楚调查中使用了哪些其他证据将利普斯与罪行关联。此案发生之际，全美各地警察部门正快速整合包括AI在内的新技术，但警方对这类工具的使用引发了关于准确性、偏见及公民自由的重要关切。利普斯的经历凸显了执法中依赖AI识别技术可能带来的严重风险与错误。

title: Stanford study reveals AI vision models invent images they never see
content: [2603.21687] MIRAGE: The Illusion of Visual Understanding Computer Science > Artificial Intelligence arXiv:2603.21687 (cs) [Submitted on 23 Mar 2026 ( v1 ), last revised 26 Mar 2026 (this version, v2)] Title: MIRAGE: The Illusion of Visual Understanding Authors: Mohammad Asadi , Jack W. O'Sullivan , Fang Cao , Tahoura Nedaee , Kamyar Fardi , Fei-Fei Li , Ehsan Adeli , Euan Ashley View a PDF of the paper titled MIRAGE: The Illusion of Visual Understanding, by Mohammad Asadi and 7 other authors View PDF HTML (experimental) Abstract: Multimodal AI systems have achieved remarkable performance across a broad range of real-world tasks, yet the mechanisms underlying visual-language reasoning remain surprisingly poorly understood. We report three findings that challenge prevailing assumptions about how these systems process and integrate visual information. First, Frontier models readily generate detailed image descriptions and elaborate reasoning traces, including pathology-biased clinical findings, for images never provided; we term this phenomenon mirage reasoning. Second, without any image input, models also attain strikingly high scores across general and medical multimodal benchmarks, bringing into question their utility and design. In the most extreme case, our model achieved the top rank on a standard chest X-ray question-answering benchmark without access to any images. Third, when models were explicitly instructed to guess answers without image access, rather than being implicitly prompted to assume images were present, performance declined markedly. Explicit guessing appears to engage a more conservative response regime, in contrast to the mirage regime in which models behave as though images have been provided. These findings expose fundamental vulnerabilities in how visual-language models reason and are evaluated, pointing to an urgent need for private benchmarks that eliminate textual cues enabling non-visual inference, particularly in medical contexts where miscalibrated AI carries the greatest consequence. We introduce B-Clean as a principled solution for fair, vision-grounded evaluation of multimodal AI systems. Subjects: Artificial Intelligence (cs.AI) Cite as: arXiv:2603.21687 [cs.AI] (or arXiv:2603.21687v2 [cs.AI] for this version) https://doi.org/10.48550/arXiv.2603.21687 Focus to learn more arXiv-issued DOI via DataCite Submission history From: Mohammad Asadi [ view email ] [v1] Mon, 23 Mar 2026 08:18:09 UTC (18,966 KB) [v2] Thu, 26 Mar 2026 07:38:26 UTC (10,637 KB) Full-text links: Access Paper: View a PDF of the paper titled MIRAGE: The Illusion of Visual Understanding, by Mohammad Asadi and 7 other authors View PDF HTML (experimental) TeX Source view license Current browse context: cs.AI < prev | next > new | recent | 2026-03 Change to browse by: cs References & Citations NASA ADS Google Scholar Semantic Scholar export BibTeX citation Loading... title_zh: 斯坦福大学研究揭示AI视觉模型会生成从未见过的图像
summary_zh: 斯坦福大学的研究团队在论文《MIRAGE：视觉理解的幻觉》中揭示，当前前沿的多模态AI系统在视觉-语言推理方面存在根本性缺陷，其表现出的“视觉理解”可能只是一种幻觉。研究提出了三个关键发现，挑战了关于这些系统如何处理和整合视觉信息的普遍假设。，首先，研究团队发现，即使在没有提供任何图像输入的情况下，前沿模型（如GPT-4、Claude等）也能轻松生成对“不存在图像”的详细描述和复杂的推理过程，甚至包括带有病理偏见的临床发现。研究者将这种现象称为“海市蜃楼式推理”（mirage reasoning）。这表明模型可能严重依赖其文本先验知识进行“脑补”，而非真正分析视觉内容。，其次，在完全没有图像输入时，这些模型在通用和医学多模态基准测试中仍能获得惊人的高分。最极端的案例是，一个模型在未接触任何图像的情况下，竟在标准的胸部X光问答基准测试中取得了最高排名。这一发现对现有多模态基准测试的效用和设计提出了根本性质疑，因为它们可能无法有效评估模型真正的视觉理解能力，反而被模型强大的文本推理能力所“欺骗”。，第三，研究对比了两种指令模式。当明确指示模型“在没有图像的情况下猜测答案”时，其性能会显著下降；而当隐含地提示模型假设图像存在时，模型则会进入“海市蜃楼”模式，表现得仿佛已收到图像。这表明，模型的响应高度依赖于提示的框架，显式猜测会触发一种更保守的响应机制。，这些发现暴露了视觉-语言模型在推理和评估方式上的根本性漏洞。论文指出，在医学等错误校准AI后果严重的领域，这一问题尤为紧迫。现有基准测试中的文本线索可能让模型进行非视觉推断，从而导致对其能力的误判。，作为解决方案，研究团队提出了B-Clean，这是一个用于对多模态AI系统进行公平、基于视觉的评估的原则性框架，旨在消除文本线索的干扰，确保评估真正检验模型的视觉基础理解能力。该研究强调了开发更严谨评估方法的迫切性，以推动构建真正可靠的多模态AI系统。

title: I am definitely missing the pre-AI writing era
content: x This website requires javascript to properly function. Consider activating javascript to get access to all site functionality. I am definitely missing the pre-AI writing era — LessWrong Personal Blog 15 I am definitely missing the pre-AI writing era by N. Cailie 29th Mar 2026 3 min read 0 15 Yesterday, I wrote my first technical draft on what I was working on with the goal to share it publicly on here (well using an account dedicated to technical post), and did not realize how wanting to sound perfect actually steal the ''my voice" in the paper. Although 80 % of the content was my own writing, the fact that it was run in a LLM enginee for grammar and vocabulary cross-check, made it failed the "probable written by AI " metric; and it was rejected. I am not complaining, well, it was kinda my fault after all cause the instructions did specify the no-use of LLM, when sending first post. And the rejection made me think: why did I even need the AI to validate what I worked on? The scary part, is I love writing, I have been writing blogs and all sort of things since I can't even remember. Have some think pieces on popular sites, and other personal blog that I used to share. And you know, before 2023, my writing was so good that I rarely had to have a second read or correction to make sure the flow stays consistent, since english is my 4th language. I always sought external review or check with tools like : Grammarly or Quilbot, for mistakes or grammars misphrasing. But now, this "superskill" has faded away, like seriously. I can't even write a 1000 words think piece, without needing or wanting to know what AI thinks or how it could have phrase it better, even for my emails. I can't write poems as before, without sounding generic, and this is mainly because I have now trained my brain to rely on these automated tools that It can not be creative anymore or think of itself. My writing has deterioted, I attempted to write a slam-poem, recently for a competition I wanted to partake in, and upon the completion when I read it, my reaction was : Who is this? Who wrote this? This is bad ! Before, I would sit and write a piece and I kid you not, the first draft was always perfect that I rarely had to write, and my best poem that got me to some "art"ies festival, was just written while I was sitting outside my hostel, with a piece of paper and a pen, and a thought that was bothering me. But now, I am slowly realizing, it has been changing in negative way. Sorry that this sounds like a rant, well, it is one ! But like, my point was to share it out loud, and kinda know if I am the only person experiencing this or if there are others? And for people who successfully taken back their creative writing skills, how did you do it? This post, is written without any tools assistance I just wrote what my brain is instructing to type (might not reread it before posting). Obviously, you would see some mistakes, some paraphrasing issue, some article misuse; I personally do no
title_zh: 我确实怀念人工智能写作时代之前的时光
summary_zh: 我确实怀念AI写作之前的时代，本文作者N. Cailie于2026年3月29日分享了一段个人体验，反思了AI工具如何影响其写作能力。作者在撰写第一篇技术草稿时，虽然80%的内容是亲自书写，但为了追求完美，使用了LLM进行语法和词汇检查，导致文章因“可能由AI生成”的指标被平台拒绝。这一经历促使作者深入思考：为何自己需要依赖AI来验证作品？，作者坦言自己热爱写作，从多年前就开始撰写博客和各类文章，英语作为其第四语言，过去写作流畅到几乎无需二次修改。在2023年之前，作者仅使用Grammarly或Quilbot等工具进行基础校对。然而如今，作者的“超能力”似乎已经消退——即使撰写一封邮件或一首诗，也忍不住想知道AI会如何改进表达，导致创作变得泛泛而谈，失去个人特色。，作者以近期尝试创作的 slam-poem 为例，完成后的第一反应是自我质疑：“这是谁写的？太糟糕了！”对比过去，作者曾仅用纸笔就在宿舍外一气呵成写出获奖诗作，如今却感到创造力被侵蚀，大脑过度依赖自动化工具，难以自主进行创造性思考。，作者将此文视为一次公开倾诉，旨在探讨是否有人面临类似困境，并向那些成功重拾创作能力的人寻求建议。本文全程未借助任何AI工具辅助，仅凭大脑指令直接书写，可能存在语法或表达瑕疵，但正体现了作者试图回归原始写作状态的决心。

title: Claude Code runs Git reset –hard origin/main against project repo every 10 mins
content: My automated tool is running git reset --hard origin/main in my project every 10 minutes · Issue #40710 · anthropics/claude-code · GitHub Skip to content You signed in with another tab or window. Reload to refresh your session. You signed out in another tab or window. Reload to refresh your session. You switched accounts on another tab or window. Reload to refresh your session. Dismiss alert anthropics / claude-code Public Notifications You must be signed in to change notification settings Fork 7.2k Star 84.5k My automated tool is running git reset --hard origin/main in my project every 10 minutes #40710 New issue Copy link New issue Copy link Closed as not planned Closed as not planned My automated tool is running git reset --hard origin/main in my project every 10 minutes #40710 Copy link Labels area:core bug Something isn't working Something isn't working data-loss platform:macos Issue specifically occurs on macOS Issue specifically occurs on macOS Description johnmathews opened on Mar 29, 2026 Issue body actions Update (2026-03-30): Root cause identified — this was NOT a Claude Code bug. The resets were caused by a separate tool I built that was running locally, which used GitPython to hard-reset the working directory on a poll cycle. The evidence was misleading because the tool shared the same CWD and monitored projects CC was working on. See my comment below for full details. Apologies for the false report. Summary Claude Code performs git fetch origin + git reset --hard origin/main on the user's project repo every 10 minutes via programmatic git operations (no external git binary spawned). This silently destroys all uncommitted changes to tracked files. Untracked files survive. Git worktrees are immune. Environment Claude Code version: 2.1.87 (Homebrew cask, compiled Bun binary) OS: macOS 15.4 (Darwin 25.3.0, arm64) Shell: zsh Evidence 1. Git reflog: 95+ entries at exact 10-minute intervals e8ea2c9 HEAD@{2026-03-29 22:19:09 +0200}: reset: moving to origin/main e8ea2c9 HEAD@{2026-03-29 22:09:09 +0200}: reset: moving to origin/main e8ea2c9 HEAD@{2026-03-29 21:59:09 +0200}: reset: moving to origin/main e8ea2c9 HEAD@{2026-03-29 21:49:09 +0200}: reset: moving to origin/main ... 8792b6c HEAD@{2026-03-29 16:55:41 +0200}: reset: moving to origin/main 8792b6c HEAD@{2026-03-29 16:45:41 +0200}: reset: moving to origin/main ... 32aa7c7 HEAD@{2026-03-28 15:47:36 +0100}: reset: moving to origin/main 32aa7c7 HEAD@{2026-03-28 15:37:36 +0100}: reset: moving to origin/main The second offset is consistent within each session but varies between sessions ( :08 , :36 , :41 , :09 ), confirming a timer tied to session start time with a 600-second interval. 95+ entries observed across 4 sessions over ~36 hours. 2. Live reproduction Modified src/lib/api.ts (tracked file) and created .canary-test.txt (untracked file) Monitored every 15 seconds At the next 10-minute mark, api.ts silently reverted — modification gone .canary-test.txt (untracked) survived Reproduced co
title_zh: Claude Code 每10分钟对项目仓库执行一次 Git reset --hard origin/main 命令
summary_zh: 用户johnmathews在Anthropic的Claude Code项目GitHub仓库提交了一个问题（#40710），报告其自动化工具每10分钟对其项目仓库执行一次`git reset --hard origin/main`操作。该问题最初被标记为涉及核心功能的bug，并标注了“数据丢失”和“macOS平台特定问题”的标签。，根据用户描述，Claude Code会通过程序化的Git操作（而非调用外部Git二进制文件）每10分钟执行一次`git fetch origin`加`git reset --hard origin/main`的组合命令。这一操作会静默地销毁所有已跟踪文件的未提交更改，但未跟踪的文件得以保留，且Git工作树不受影响。，用户提供了详细证据：Git引用日志显示超过95条记录，以精确的10分钟间隔记录了重置操作，时间跨度为约36小时内的4个会话。现场复现也证实，在10分钟标记点时，已跟踪的修改文件会被静默恢复，而未跟踪的测试文件则幸存。，然而，在2026年3月30日的更新中，用户确认根本原因并非Claude Code的bug。问题根源在于用户本地运行的另一个独立工具，该工具使用GitPython库，在一个轮询周期内对工作目录执行了硬重置。误导性的证据源于该工具与Claude Code共享相同当前工作目录并监控相同项目。用户为此错误报告致歉，随后该问题被关闭并标记为“未计划修复”。，此事件凸显了在复杂开发环境中，当多个自动化工具同时操作同一代码库时，准确诊断问题根源的挑战性。

Vibing , a voice-powered input method, is now built on top of VibeVoice-ASR. Download: macOS | Windows 1.mov 2026-03-06: 🚀 VibeVoice ASR is now part of a Transformers release ! You can now use our speech recognition model directly through the Hugging Face Transformers library for seamless integration into your projects. 2026-01-21: 📣 We open-sourced VibeVoice-ASR , a unified speech-to-text model designed to handle 60-minute long-form audio in a single pass, generating structured transcriptions containing Who (Speaker), When (Timestamps), and What (Content), with support for User-Customized Context. Try it in Playground . ⭐️ VibeVoice-ASR is natively multilingual, supporting over 50 languages — check the supported languages for details. 🔥 The VibeVoice-ASR finetuning code is now available! ⚡️ vLLM inference is now supported for faster inference; see vllm-asr for more details. 📑 VibeVoice-ASR Technique Report is available. 2025-12-16: 📣 We added experimental speakers to VibeVoice‑Realtime‑0.5B for exploration, including multilingual voices in nine languages (DE, FR, IT, JP, KR, NL, PL, PT, ES) and 11 distinct English style voices. Try it . More speaker types will be added over time. 2025-12-03: 📣 We open-sourced VibeVoice‑Realtime‑0.5B , a real‑time text‑to‑speech model that supports streaming text input and robust long-form speech generation. Try it on Colab . 2025-09-05: VibeVoice is an open-source research framework intended to advance collaboration in the speech synthesis community. After release, we discovered instances where the tool was used in ways inconsistent with the stated intent. Since responsible use of AI is one of Microsoft’s guiding principles, we have removed the VibeVoice-TTS code from this repository. 2025-08-25: 📣 We open-sourced VibeVoice-TTS , a long-form multi-speaker text-to-speech model that can synthesize speech up to 90 minutes long with up to 4 distinct speakers. Overview VibeVoice is a family of open-sour
title_zh: 微软/VibeVoice - 开源前沿语音人工智能
summary_zh: 微软开源项目VibeVoice是一系列前沿语音AI模型，旨在推动语音合成社区的合作研究。该项目包含多个核心组件，最新进展包括VibeVoice-ASR语音识别模型和VibeVoice-Realtime-0.5B实时文本转语音模型。，VibeVoice-ASR是一个统一的语音转文本模型，其突出特点是能够单次处理长达60分钟的长音频，并生成包含说话人身份、时间戳和内容的结构化转录。该模型原生支持超过50种语言，并允许用户自定义上下文以提升识别准确性。技术方面，它已集成至Hugging Face Transformers库，方便开发者直接调用；同时支持vLLM推理以加速处理。微调代码和详细技术报告也已公开。，VibeVoice-Realtime-0.5B是一个实时文本转语音模型，支持流式文本输入和稳健的长语音生成。该模型已添加实验性说话人扩展，包括九种多语言语音（德、法、意、日、韩、荷、波、葡、西）以及11种独特的英语风格语音。，项目历史显示，VibeVoice最初于2025年8月开源了VibeVoice-TTS，这是一个长文本多说话人语音合成模型，可合成长达90分钟、最多包含4位不同说话人的语音。但出于对AI负责任使用的考虑，微软已于2025年9月将该TTS代码从仓库移除。，目前，VibeVoice-ASR已被开源社区采用，例如语音输入法Vibing就基于该模型构建。项目提供了在线Playground和Colab演示，供用户快速体验。整体而言，VibeVoice通过开源高性能的语音识别与合成模型，为开发者和研究者提供了强大的工具链。

A "hello world" slash command doesn't help you build a production code review pipeline that uses memory, delegates to specialized agents, and runs security scans automatically. You're leaving
title_zh: luongnv89/claude-howto - 从基础概念到高级智能体，一份可视化、实例驱动的Claude代码指南，内含可直接复用的模板助您快速实现价值。
summary_zh: GitHub项目“luongnv89/claude-howto”是一个针对Claude Code的视觉化、示例驱动的综合指南。该项目旨在帮助用户从基础概念快速过渡到掌握高级代理（agents）的构建与使用，其核心价值在于提供了大量可直接复制粘贴的模板，让开发者能立即获得实用价值。，该项目系统性地解决了学习Claude Code时的常见痛点：官方文档往往只孤立介绍功能，而缺乏如何将这些功能（如斜杠命令、记忆、钩子、子代理、MCP服务器等）组合成高效工作流的实战指导。本指南通过结构化的学习路径，明确了从入门到精通的学习顺序，避免了用户盲目摸索。，项目内容组织清晰，涵盖了从01斜杠命令、02记忆、03技能、04子代理、05 MCP（模型上下文协议）、06钩子、07插件、08检查点、09高级功能到10命令行界面（CLI）等核心模块。它不仅仅提供“Hello World”式的简单示例，而是展示了如何构建可用于生产环境的复杂工作流，例如一个能利用记忆功能、委托给专门代理并自动运行安全扫描的代码审查管道。，该资源库提供了快速入门指南、功能目录、学习路线图以及丰富的视觉教程，承诺让用户能在一个周末内掌握Claude Code。其目标是使用户从简单的提示词输入者，成长为能够协调智能体、技能和MCP服务器的熟练开发者，从而真正节省开发时间。项目在GitHub上已获得大量关注，拥有超过9.1k的星标，表明了其在开发者社区中的实用性和受欢迎程度。

Everything else is automatic. Not Sure Where to Start? It exposes hidd
title_zh: Yeachan-Heo/oh-my-claudecode - 面向团队的Claude Code多智能体编排平台
summary_zh: GitHub项目oh-my-claudecode是一个专为Claude Code设计的团队优先多智能体编排框架。该项目旨在简化开发流程，让用户无需深入学习Claude Code即可快速上手，实现零学习曲线。其核心价值在于通过多智能体协作，自动化处理复杂的编码任务，提升团队开发效率。，项目提供了两种安装方式：推荐大多数Claude Code用户通过Marketplace插件安装，也可通过npm CLI全局安装。安装后，用户只需运行简单的设置命令，即可开始构建项目。例如，通过输入“autopilot: build a REST API for managing tasks”指令，系统便能自动完成相关开发工作。，oh-my-claudecode的一个关键特性是深度访谈功能。当用户需求不明确时，可使用“/deep-interview”命令（例如“/deep-interview 'I want to build a task management app'”），系统会通过苏格拉底式提问帮助澄清思路，在编写代码前暴露隐藏的假设和需求，从而确保项目方向正确。，该项目支持多语言文档，拥有活跃的社区和详细的资源，包括入门指南、CLI参考、工作流说明和迁移指南。它通过智能体分工协作，将开发任务分解并由不同专长的AI代理处理，实现了从需求分析到代码生成的自动化编排，显著降低了使用Claude Code进行复杂项目开发的门槛。

Automatic tracking of file edits with rewind ( Esc Esc or /rewind ) and targeted summarization CLI Startup Flags claude [flags] Command-line flags, subcommands, and environment variables for launching Claude Code · Interactive Mode AI Terms Agentic Engineering · Context Engineering · Vibe Coding Best
summary_zh: GitHub项目“claude-code-best-practice”是一个专注于提升Claude代码助手使用效率与效果的最佳实践集合库。其核心理念是“实践使Claude臻于完美”，旨在通过系统化的方法和工具配置，帮助开发者充分发挥Claude在编程辅助方面的潜力。，项目围绕几个关键概念构建了一套完整的架构体系。首先是“子代理”（Subagents），它们被定义为在独立上下文中运行的自主执行者，可以拥有自定义工具、权限、模型、记忆和持久身份。其次是“命令”（Commands），作为注入现有上下文的知识，它们是用户可调用的简单提示模板，用于编排工作流。第三是“技能”（Skills），同样是注入上下文的知识，但具备可配置、可预加载、可自动发现的特点，并支持上下文分叉和渐进式披露。，项目还集成了工作流编排、事件钩子（Hooks）以及Model Context Protocol（MCP）服务器连接，用于对接外部工具、数据库和API。其配置系统具有层次结构，涵盖权限管理、模型配置、输出风格、沙箱环境、快捷键和快速模式等。状态栏可自定义显示上下文使用情况、模型、成本和会话信息。，记忆系统通过CLAUDE.md文件、规则和项目特定记忆目录实现持久化上下文管理，并支持自动记忆和基于Git的自动检查点功能。该项目也倡导“智能体工程”、“上下文工程”和“氛围编码”等AI开发理念。，总体而言，该项目提供了一个模块化、可扩展的框架，通过定义清晰的模式（如代理、命令、技能）和丰富的配置选项，使开发者能够更精细地控制和优化Claude在代码生成、问题解答和工作流自动化中的行为，从而提升开发体验与生产力。

title: OpenBB-finance/OpenBB - Financial data platform for analysts, quants and AI agents.
content: GitHub - OpenBB-finance/OpenBB: Financial data platform for analysts, quants and AI agents. · GitHub Skip to content You signed in with another tab or window. Reload to refresh your session. You signed out in another tab or window. Reload to refresh your session. You switched accounts on another tab or window. Reload to refresh your session. Dismiss alert OpenBB-finance / OpenBB Public Notifications You must be signed in to change notification settings Fork 6.3k Star 64.3k develop Branches Tags Go to file Code Open more actions menu Folders and files Name Name Last commit message Last commit date Latest commit History 6,825 Commits 6,825 Commits .github .github assets assets build build cli cli cookiecutter cookiecutter desktop desktop examples examples frontend-components frontend-components images images openbb_platform openbb_platform .codespell.ignore .codespell.ignore .codespell.skip .codespell.skip .coveragerc .coveragerc .gitattributes .gitattributes .gitignore .gitignore .markdownlintrc .markdownlintrc .pre-commit-config.yaml .pre-commit-config.yaml .pylintrc .pylintrc .secrets.baseline .secrets.baseline CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md LICENSE LICENSE README.md README.md SECURITY.md SECURITY.md pytest.ini pytest.ini ruff.toml ruff.toml View all files Repository files navigation Open Data Platform by OpenBB (ODP) is the open-source toolset that helps data engineers integrate proprietary, licensed, and public data sources into downstream applications like AI copilots and research dashboards. ODP operates as the "connect once, consume everywhere" infrastructure layer that consolidates and exposes data to multiple surfaces at once: Python environments for quants, OpenBB Workspace and Excel for analysts, MCP servers for AI agents, and REST APIs for other applications. Get started with: pip install openbb from openbb import obb output = obb . equity . price . historical ( "AAPL" ) df = output . to_dataframe () Data integrations available can be found here: https://docs.openbb.co/python/reference OpenBB Workspace While the Open Data Platform provides the open-source data integration foundation, OpenBB Workspace offers the enterprise UI for analysts to visualize datasets and leverage AI agents. The platform's "connect once, consume everywhere" architecture enables seamless integration between the two. You can find OpenBB Workspace at https://pro.openbb.co . Data integration: You can learn more about adding data to the OpenBB workspace from the docs or this open source repository . AI Agents integration: You can learn more about adding AI agents to the OpenBB workspace from this open source repository . Integrating Open Data Platform to the OpenBB Workspace Connect this library to the OpenBB Workspace with a few simple commands, in a Python (3.9.21 - 3.12) environment. Run an ODP backend Install the packages. pip install " openbb[all] " Start the API server over localhost. openbb-api This will launch a FastAPI server, via Uvicorn, at 127.0.0
title_zh: OpenBB-finance/OpenBB - 面向分析师、量化交易员和AI代理的金融数据平台
summary_zh: OpenBB是一个面向金融分析师、量化交易员和AI代理的开源金融数据平台。其核心是Open Data Platform (ODP)，这是一个开源工具集，旨在帮助数据工程师将专有、授权和公共数据源集成到下游应用中，例如AI副驾驶和研究仪表板。，ODP采用“一次连接，随处消费”的基础架构设计，作为统一的数据层，能够将整合后的数据同时暴露给多个使用界面：包括面向量化分析师的Python环境、面向分析师的OpenBB Workspace和Excel、面向AI代理的MCP服务器，以及面向其他应用的REST API。用户可以通过简单的Python命令快速开始使用，例如安装`openbb`包后，调用`obb.equity.price.historical("AAPL")`即可获取苹果公司的历史股价数据并转换为DataFrame。，该平台提供了丰富的数据集成，覆盖股票、宏观经济、加密货币等多种金融数据类型，具体信息可在其官方文档中查询。，与底层ODP相对应的是OpenBB Workspace，这是一个为企业用户提供的可视化界面。分析师可以在此平台上直观地查看数据集并利用集成的AI代理进行分析。ODP与Workspace通过上述架构无缝连接，实现了数据基础设施与前端应用的高效协同。，用户可以通过简单的步骤将ODP后端连接到Workspace。在Python 3.9至3.12环境中，使用`pip install "openbb[all]"`安装完整包后，运行`openbb-api`命令即可在本地启动一个基于FastAPI和Uvicorn的服务器，为Workspace提供数据服务。，总的来说，OpenBB平台通过其开源的数据集成平台ODP和商业化的Workspace界面，构建了一个灵活、统一的金融数据生态系统，旨在服务于从量化研究到AI驱动分析等多种金融工作流程。

It's the only agent with a built-in learning loop — it creates skills from experience, improves them during use, nudges itself to persist knowledge, searches its own past conversations, and builds a deepening model of who you are across sessions. Run it on a $5 VPS, a GPU cluster, or serverless infrastructure that costs nearly nothing when idle. It's not tied to your laptop — talk to it from Telegram while it works on a cloud VM. Use any model you want — Nous Portal , OpenRouter (200+ models), z.ai/GLM , Kimi/Moonshot , MiniMax , OpenAI, or your own endpoint. Switch with hermes model — no code changes, no lock-in. A real terminal interface Full TUI with multiline editing, slash-command aut
title_zh: NousResearch/hermes-agent - 与你共同成长的智能体
summary_zh: NousResearch在GitHub上开源了Hermes Agent项目，这是一个具备自我进化能力的AI智能体框架。其核心特点是内置了“学习循环”机制，能够从交互经验中自主创建并优化技能，在运行过程中持续改进，主动沉淀知识，检索历史对话记录，并跨会话构建不断深化的用户画像。，该框架设计灵活，支持从低成本VPS（如5美元/月实例）、GPU集群到闲置时近乎零成本的Serverless基础设施等多种部署环境。Agent可运行于云端虚拟机，用户能通过Telegram等平台远程交互，摆脱本地设备束缚。，在模型支持上，Hermes Agent具备高度开放性，兼容包括Nous Portal、OpenRouter（超200个模型）、智谱AI/GLM、Kimi/Moonshot、MiniMax、OpenAI及私有化端点在内的多种LLM服务。用户可通过`hermes model`命令自由切换模型，无需修改代码，有效避免了供应商锁定。，项目提供了完整的终端用户界面（TUI），支持多行编辑和斜杠命令自动补全等交互功能。其架构允许Agent通过经验积累生成新技能，并在使用中持续优化这些技能，同时具备自我提示以固化知识、搜索过往对话的能力，从而实现跨会话的个性化能力成长。，技术栈上，项目采用Python开发，提供了Docker支持、详细的配置示例和完整的API文档。作为由Nous Research构建的开源项目，它旨在创建一个能够伴随用户需求共同成长、具备记忆和持续学习能力的AI智能体系统。
